items.title: Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking (PDF)
items.author: assets.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers in early 2026. The most recent include 'Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model' (April 2, 2026), examining whether Claude has functional emotional representations and can introspect on its internal states. On the safety and alignment front, papers on 'Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking in Production RL' and 'Alignment Faking in Large Language Models' explore concerning emergent behaviors in models under

items.title: Anthropic Mythos AI model revealed in data leak — Fortune
items.author: fortune.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a busy early 2026 with major model releases. On February 5, 2026, they released Claude Opus 4.6, and followed up with Claude Sonnet 4.6 on February 17, 2026. Both models feature significant upgrades across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, agent planning, knowledge work, and design — with a 1M token context window available in beta. New capabilities rolled out alongside these releases include computer use (allowing Claude to open files, click, and navigate screens),

items.title: Anthropic Release Notes - April 2026 Latest Updates
items.author: releasebot.io
items.snippet: Anthropic has released several significant API updates in March-April 2026. The most notable is the launch of Claude Sonnet 4.6, their latest balanced model with improved agentic search performance, extended thinking support, and a 1M token context window at standard pricing. The Message Batches API received an enhancement allowing max_tokens up to 300k for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 via the output-300k-2026-03-24 beta header, enabling longer outputs for code generation and long-form content

items.title: Anthropic News | Home
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's recent Twitter/X announcements and news in early April 2026 center on several major developments. Most notably, Anthropic confirmed it is testing a powerful new AI model internally called 'Claude Mythos,' described as a 'step change' in capability and the most capable model the company has built to date.
